title: Size of a Signal
summary: This page presents Example 1.1, which classifies two signals as either energy or power signals. The first signal decays to zero as |t| approaches infinity, so its energy is computed as 8 joules. The second signal is periodic and does not decay, so its power is computed by averaging over one period, yielding Px = 1/3 and an rms value of 1/√3.
